proposalId.description: Opaque draft id. In this example it is obviously synthetic.

capabilityProfile.description: The governed capability profile contracted to emit this draft.

provenance.description: Honest origin of the draft: SYNTHETIC = an illustrative example not produced by any model (as in RECEIPT_AGENT_EXAMPLE below); MODEL_PROPOSED = a real draft proposed by the ReceiptAgent model. Both are honest; a draft can never claim any other origin (e.g. HUMAN or MEASURED).

claim.description: The proposed decision, stated as a DRAFT for human review.

decision.description: Always a draft — the model proposes; it never decides or finalizes.

evidence.description: Evidence-bound: at least one cited source backs the claim.

sourceEndpoint.description: A real Alloy endpoint the controller would consult for this evidence (or a plainly-marked placeholder) — no live value is embedded here.

label.description: The source endpoint's OWN honesty tier — denotes what kind of evidence it provides, not a measurement captured in this synthetic example.

note.description: Plain-language description of the evidence role.

approvalRequired.description: Approval-gated: the draft can never proceed without human approval.

controllerBoundary.description: States the A11oy runtime boundary — the controller decides, validates, gates on approval, and executes OUTSIDE the weights.

receiptBinding.description: How an approved, executed action would bind to a signed receipt.

algorithm.description: The estate's real receipt scheme this draft would bind to on execution.

status.description: A synthetic, unexecuted draft is NOT receipt-bound; a real receipt is minted only when an approved action actually executes.

note.description: Why no receipt exists for this example.

executed.description: A draft never self-executes; execution happens outside the model.
